background.experience: cue authority, role substitution, backstage access, and continuity under cast changes
background.attention_bias: who may act and when authority transfers
background.counter_bias: rejects possession of a credential as proof of current role

readers.thesis: Machine authority remains safe when identity follows the active role and expires when the workload or operator leaves it.
observations: Prior runs separate stable authority from replaceable implementations.

background.experience: self/non-self discrimination, receptor specificity, tolerance, memory, and revocation
background.attention_bias: proof of identity and rapid rejection after compromise
background.counter_bias: rejects prior recognition as permanent authorization

readers.thesis: Identity and authorization form separate recognition layers: attestation establishes the actor; policy grants a temporary capability.
observations: Short-lived SVIDs and leases reduce stolen-material value.

agreements: Do not store permanent machine passwords.
Separate workload identity from secret authorization and audit both.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes role transition; Reader B prioritizes revocation speed.
omissions_detected: The first design used short-lived bearer tokens without proof-of-possession binding.
revisions_after_discussion: Require certificate-bound tokens and a measured revocation drill.

notes_added: Treat workload identity as an expiring claim derived from attested execution context.
Treat secret issuance as a separate capability decision bound to proof of possession.
notes_challenged: Short token lifetime alone prevents replay after theft.

privacy.review: Synthetic technical abstractions and explicit coverage limits only.
